title: Critonia peninsularis
taxonomy: Critonia peninsularis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Critonia peninsularis is a member of the genus Critonia, which is part of the tribe Anthemideae. The plant is also known as Desert broomweed or Broomweed. Its classification is as a dicotyledonous perennial herb.

The health benefits of Critonia peninsularis include its ability to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
